_comment: PoE Delve Fossil reference. Each fossil adds certain mod tags and blocks others, shaping the outcome of fossil crafting.
_note: adds_tags = mod categories this fossil makes more likely. blocks_tags = mod categories this fossil prevents entirely. min_depth = approximate minimum Delve depth to find this fossil.

fossils.name: Pristine Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll life and energy shield modifiers. Cannot roll attack modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Guarantee life or ES mods on any base. Best for: life bases (body armour, helm, boots), ES bases. Combine with Dense for pure life+defence.
fossils.notes: One of the highest-value fossils. Pristine + Dense is a classic life/armour crafting combo.

fossils.name: Scorched Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll fire damage and fire resistance modifiers. Cannot roll cold modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Stack fire damage on weapons or fire resistance on armour. Useful for fire-damage builds targeting +fire pen or high fire res.

fossils.name: Frigid Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll cold damage and cold resistance modifiers. Cannot roll fire modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Stack cold damage on weapons or cold resistance on armour. Pairs with Metallic to block fire+chaos from jewellery.

fossils.name: Metallic Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll lightning damage and lightning resistance modifiers. Cannot roll chaos modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Stack lightning damage on weapons. Blocking chaos mods is useful when chaos res on the base is undesirable for multimod crafting.

fossils.name: Jagged Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll physical damage and physical defence modifiers. Cannot roll lightning modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Best for stacking physical damage on weapons. Combine with Serrated for high physical-attack weapons. Common for BV/Lacerate builds.

fossils.name: Aberrant Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll chaos damage and chaos resistance modifiers. Cannot roll physical modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Stack chaos damage on weapons or chaos resistance on armour. Useful for Chaos builds (Volkuur's, Poison builds, BV poison variants).

fossils.name: Serrated Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll attack modifiers (attack speed, accuracy, on-hit effects). Cannot roll life modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Stacking attack speed, accuracy, and on-hit effects on weapons. Note: blocks life, so avoid on items needing life mods.
fossils.notes: Strong for pure DPS weapons where life is irrelevant (e.g., 2H weapons used with HP stacking elsewhere).

fossils.name: Bound Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll minion modifiers (minion damage, minion speed, minion life). Cannot roll elemental modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Essential for Necromancer and minion build gear. Bound + Pristine for life + minion mods on wands/sceptres/helmets.

fossils.name: Lucent Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll mana modifiers (mana, mana regeneration, mana cost reduction). Cannot roll physical modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: For mana-stacking builds (Archmage, Hierophant). Combine with Pristine for life+mana on body armour.

fossils.name: Dense Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll defence modifiers (armour, evasion, energy shield values). Cannot roll elemental modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Stack pure defence values (armour, evasion, ES) on items. Dense + Pristine is the classic armour+life crafting combo.
fossils.notes: Blocking elemental mods means no resistances — plan accordingly.

fossils.name: Corroded Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll ailment modifiers (chance to ignite/bleed/poison, ailment damage). Cannot roll life modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Ailment builds (ignite Elementalist, bleed Gladiator, poison Pathfinder). Avoid when life is needed.

fossils.name: Aetheric Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll AoE modifiers (AoE radius, AoE damage). Cannot roll physical modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: AoE spellcaster weapons and wands. Less commonly used; AoE mods are relatively narrow pool.

fossils.name: Shuddering Fossil
fossils.effect: Can roll mods that normally cannot appear together (conflicting tag rules ignored). Produces unusual mod combinations, some extremely powerful.
fossils.crafting_use: Expensive crafting for unusual synergies. Used when targeting specific mod combos that normally cannot coexist. Very RNG.
fossils.notes: Most valuable when seeking specific conflicting combinations. High variance — can produce mirror-tier items or complete bricks.

fossils.name: Perfect Fossil
fossils.effect: Sets item quality to exactly 30% (maximum possible quality without quality crafting). No other changes to modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Pre-crafting step to maximise quality before rolling mods. Essential for weapons (higher physical DPS) and flasks.
fossils.notes: Apply before any other crafting. Quality affects implicit calculations for some bases. Only use on a white (normal) item.

fossils.name: Gilded Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll item quantity and item rarity modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Rolling IIQ/IIR on flasks (for MF characters) or MF gear bases. Niche but useful for dedicated MF builds.

fossils.name: Prismatic Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll elemental resistance modifiers (fire, cold, lightning, all resistances).
fossils.crafting_use: Targeting high resistance rolls on rings, belts, and body armour. Useful for budget crafting or resistance-stacking builds.
fossils.notes: One of the easier fossils for beginners — straightforward application and doesn't block anything.

fossils.name: Fundamental Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll elemental damage modifiers (added fire/cold/lightning damage). Cannot roll physical modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Elemental wand or sceptre crafting for hybrid elemental spellcasters. Also useful for jewellery with multiple elemental damage mods.

fossils.name: Deft Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll speed modifiers (attack speed, cast speed, movement speed). Cannot roll life modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Stacking cast speed on wands or attack speed on weapons. Combine with Serrated for maximum attack speed weapons.

fossils.name: Faceted Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll gem level modifiers (+N to [gem name], +N to [skill type] gems). Cannot roll speed modifiers.
fossils.crafting_use: Targeting +1 to specific skills or +2 to a gem type on wands, sceptres, staves. Essential for spellcaster wand crafting.
fossils.notes: High depth required. Extremely valuable — Faceted + Pristine for +gems and ES on wands/sceptres is a top-tier combo.

fossils.name: Fractured Fossil
fossils.effect: Randomly fractures one modifier on the item, making it permanently fixed. The fractured mod cannot be removed by crafting.
fossils.crafting_use: Lock in a perfect desired mod before multimodding. Extremely powerful — fracturing T1 life or a top DPS mod enables cost-effective crafting.
fossils.notes: Applies to magic or rare items. The fractured mod is random — have multiple good mods on the item first for best results.

fossils.name: Bloodstained Fossil
fossils.effect: More likely to roll item drop quantity/rarity modifiers and monster-drop-related effects.
fossils.crafting_use: MF (magic find) gear crafting. Used for headhunter-adjacent setups or dedicated IIQ belt crafting.

fossils.name: Sanctified Fossil
fossils.effect: All numeric values on rolled modifiers are biased toward the higher end of their tier range. Does not change which mods appear — only the roll values.
fossils.crafting_use: Maximising mod values on already-good items. Extremely valuable late in crafting to get top-roll values (e.g., T1 flat life rolling maximum 99 rather than 80).
fossils.notes: Does not guarantee top-roll, but dramatically increases probability. Best used after locking desired mods via fracture or harvest.

fossils.name: Glyphic Fossil
fossils.effect: Can roll enchantment-style modifiers that are normally only obtainable via the Labyrinth enchantment system.
fossils.crafting_use: Targeting lab-style enchants (e.g., skill effect enchants) without running the Lab. Very rare and valuable.
fossils.notes: Very deep delve required. Enchant pool is narrow; combine with targeted crafting for specific skills.

fossils.name: Hollow Fossil
fossils.effect: Creates an abyssal socket on the item (a special socket type that accepts Abyss Jewels). Item can have fewer regular sockets as a result.
fossils.crafting_use: Adding abyssal sockets to gear for Abyss jewels builds (Darkness Enthroned, Abyss Jewel stacking).
fossils.notes: Extremely rare. The only way to add an abyssal socket outside of Abyss items. Very deep delve required.

fossils.name: Tangled Fossil
fossils.effect: Can roll corrupted-implicit-style modifiers or combinations of mods from wildly different tag groups. May produce extremely powerful or nonsensical combinations.
fossils.crafting_use: Ultra-high-end crafting to hit combinations impossible by other means. Very unpredictable.
fossils.notes: Extremely rare and chaotic. Used by expert crafters chasing mirror-tier combinations. Not for beginners.

Primitive Alchemical Resonator.note: 1 fossil slot. Common, found at any depth.

Potent Alchemical Resonator.note: 2 fossil slots. Found at moderate depths.

Powerful Alchemical Resonator.note: 3 fossil slots. Found at deeper depths.

Prime Alchemical Resonator.note: 4 fossil slots. Rare, requires deep delving.

tips: Fossils work by re-rolling a rare item (like an Orb of Alchemy). The item must be white (normal rarity) or magic before applying.
Multiple fossils in a resonator stack their add/block effects. All blocks apply simultaneously.
Fractured Fossil is best used on an item that already has 1–2 excellent mods from other crafting methods.
Perfect Fossil should always be the first step — apply to a white item before any other crafting.
Sanctified Fossil is most valuable when you already have the right mods and want to maximize their numeric values.
